files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Engine.DataflowStreamBuilder

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.FlowtideHealth

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.FlowtidePauseOptions

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.IRentable

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.IVertexHandler

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.LongWatermarkValue

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.TriggerEvent

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Utils.SingleAsyncEnumerable<T>
files.Summary: A single-allocation async enumerable that yields exactly one element of type T.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.EgressVertex<T>

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.FixedPointVertex<T>

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.IStreamEgressVertex
files.Summary: Represents an egress node (sink operator) within the Flowtide dataflow stream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.IStreamIngressVertex

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.IStreamVertex
files.Summary: Represents a foundational node (operator or vertex) within the Flowtide dataflow stream. Defines lifecycle methods (Setup, Initialize, CreateBlock, Link), operational hooks (Pause, Resume, Compact), and core metadata properties.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.IngressOutput<T>
files.Summary: Provides an output for an to push data and watermarks into the stream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.IngressVertex<TData>
files.Summary: Base class for stream ingress vertices (sources) that produce stream events into the dataflow pipeline.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.MultipleInputTargetHolder
files.Summary: Holder that represents one input slot of a , implementing so that upstream sources can be linked directly to it.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.MultipleInputVertex<T>
files.Summary: Vertex capable of receiving and processing input from multiple distinct upstream sources concurrently.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.PartitionVertex<T>
files.Summary: Vertex responsible for partitioning an input stream into multiple target streams.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.PartitionedOutputVertex<T>
files.Summary: Represents a specialized vertex that gathers data from multiple upstream partitions and merges them back into a single continuous stream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Vertices.UnaryVertex<T>
files.Summary: Base class for stream vertices that have a single input stream and one or more output streams.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.Watermark
files.Summary: Event that contains watermarks from the sources. Each source has unique watermarks to allow the egress to keep track of which offset from each source it is finished with.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Base.WatermarkSerializeFactory
files.Summary: Provides a global registry mapping integer type IDs to implementations. This allows custom watermark types to be correctly serialized and deserialized across the stream engine.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.FlowtideOrleansOptions
files.Summary: Options for streams hosted in Orleans.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Interfaces.ISubStreamGrain
files.Summary: Grain running one substream. Most methods are the internal protocol between substream grains; don't call them from application code or manage a single substream directly, use .

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Internal.SubstreamEventsPayloadCodec
files.Summary: Serializes only when a message actually crosses a silo boundary: Orleans invokes codecs at transport time, so grains on the same silo never pay for serialization. Writes the substream wire format (columnar, batch level) directly into the message and releases the events' receiver claims on the sending side; reading captures the raw payload into pooled segments without materializing the events, the communication handler deserializes lazily with the consuming operator's allocator.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Internal.SubstreamEventsPayloadCopier
files.Summary: The payload transfers ownership of the events with the reference; grains on the same silo hand the live events over uncopied. A deep copy would double the receiver claims.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Messages.StreamStatusResponse
files.Summary: Status of a stream and all substreams it started.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Messages.SubstreamEventsPayload
files.Summary:
Fetched substream events, serialized only when they actually cross a silo boundary.

The serving grain hands the live events over by reference; between grains on the same silo the payload instance passes through Orleans uncopied (identity copier) and the receiver consumes the live events with zero copies, taking over the receiver claim exactly like a local fetch. Only when Orleans serializes the message does the codec write the events in the substream wire format, releasing the claims on the sending side; the receiving silo captures the raw payload without materializing it, and the communication handler deserializes lazily with the consuming operator's allocator.

Ownership is one-shot: exactly one consumer takes the live events (the same-silo receiver or the serializing codec). A payload is bound to one response.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Cluster.Orleans.Messages.SubstreamStatus
files.Summary: Status of one substream, reported by its substream grain.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.DeltaLake.Internal.Delta.DeletionVectors.RoaringBitmap.RoaringBitmap
files.Summary: Code from https://github.com/Tornhoof/RoaringBitmap/tree/master which is archived

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.Files.Internal.CsvFiles.CsvFileInternalOptions
files.Summary: Internal options class with alot of the properties as required for easy null handling.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbConsistency
files.Summary: Represents the consistency requirements for a SpiceDB API request, controlling how data freshness is balanced against latency.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbContextualizedCaveat
files.Summary: Represents a caveat attached to a caveated relationship in SpiceDB, combining a caveat expression name with its evaluation context.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbObjectReference
files.Summary: Represents a reference to a specific object in the SpiceDB permission system, identified by its type and unique identifier.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbPrecondition
files.Summary: Represents a precondition that must be satisfied before a SpiceDB write operation is committed.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbReadRelationshipsRequest
files.Summary: Represents a request to read relationships from SpiceDB that match one or more filters.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbRelationFilter
files.Summary: Represents a filter on the sub-relation of a subject reference within a SpiceDB subject filter.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbRelationship
files.Summary: Represents a SpiceDB relationship that specifies how a resource relates to a subject.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbRelationshipFilter
files.Summary: Represents a collection of filters that, when applied to a set of relationships, return only those with exactly matching fields.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbRelationshipUpdate
files.Summary: Represents a mutation of a single relationship in SpiceDB, combining the operation to perform with the relationship to act upon.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbSubjectFilter
files.Summary: Represents a filter on the subject side of a relationship in SpiceDB.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbSubjectReference
files.Summary: Represents a reference to the subject portion of a SpiceDB relationship, identifying a subject by its object reference and an optional sub-relation.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbWriteRelationshipRequest
files.Summary: Represents a request to atomically apply a set of relationship mutations to SpiceDB, with optional preconditions.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.ISpiceDbZedToken
files.Summary: Represents a ZedToken that encodes causality metadata for SpiceDB read and write operations.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.SpiceDbPreconditionOperation
files.Summary: Specifies how the existence or absence of relationships matching a filter determines whether a SpiceDB write operation proceeds.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.SpiceDbRelationshipUpdateOperation
files.Summary: Specifies the mutation operation to perform on a relationship in a SpiceDB write request.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.SpiceDbSinkOptions
files.Summary: Provides configuration options for the SpiceDB relationship sink connector.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.SpiceDbSourceOptions
files.Summary: Provides configuration options for the SpiceDB relationship source connector.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Connector.SpiceDB.SpiceDbToFlowtide
files.Summary: Provides a utility method for converting a SpiceDB permission schema into a Flowtide query plan that computes the effective subjects for a given resource type and relation.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.ColumnStore.Column
files.Summary:
Represents a column of data.

It has the following state machine: Start -> Null column Add null value -> Null column Add value -> Value column (single type) Add different type -> Union column

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.ColumnStore.Comparers.EventBatchDataComparer
files.Summary: Comparers two event batches for equality, used for unit testing

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.ColumnStore.DataValueContainer
files.Summary: Container used to avoid boxing as much as possible. Caller can allocate this once on the heap and call GetValueAt passing in this container to avoid boxing.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.ColumnStore.Serialization.EventBatchDeserializer
files.Summary: Deserializer that aims to not allocate any extra memory except for the buffers that are needed to store the data.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Compute.Columnar.ITableFunctionOutput
files.Summary: Sink that a compiled table function appends its generated rows into.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Compute.Columnar.TableFunctionEmit
files.Summary: A compiled table function. For input row index in batch it appends the produced rows directly into output.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Connectors.IConnectorSourceFactory
files.Summary:
Factory for creating source connectors. First CanHandle is called, if the factory responds true, then ModifyPlan is called. After that, CanHandle is called again, if it returns true, then CreateSource is called.

This is done since the plan may be modified to include multiple read relations in case of partitions. So each new read relation will have to be called with CanHandle and CreateSource.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distributed.DistributedFlowtideStream
files.Summary: A distributed stream where all substreams run in the same process. Built with .

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distributed.DistributedStreamBuilder
files.Summary: Builds a distributed stream where all substreams run in the same process. The plan may already contain substreams (e.g. from SQL SUBSTREAM) or be split with ; each substream runs as its own stream with its own storage and they communicate through a .

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Engine.Distributed.LocalSubstreamCommunicationHub
files.Summary: Connects substreams that run in the same process. Each gets its own from ; messages are dispatched directly to the callbacks the other substream registered.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.IRowData
files.Summary: Contains the data of a row

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Lineage.OpenLineageHttpOptions
files.Summary: Configuration options for reporting OpenLineage events over HTTP.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Lineage.TableLineageMetadata
files.Summary: Represents lineage metadata for a table, used to describe a dataset in an OpenLineage context. Contains the namespace, table name, and optional schema that together identify a dataset and its structure for lineage tracking.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Exchange.SubstreamEventWireSerializer
files.Summary:
Wire format for events fetched between substreams on different nodes. The payload is a single opaque byte stream, so any transport (Orleans grain calls, an actor framework) can move it, using the same columnar encoding the exchange queues spill with (no per-row work).

Serialize writes into an and Deserialize reads from a , so pooled segmented buffers (e.g. Orleans PooledBuffer) move events without intermediate byte arrays. Serialize never allocates batch memory; Deserialize allocates from the target's resolved allocator and references nothing in the payload, so the transport may release it once Deserialize returns.

Rent ownership: the receiver claim from the dequeue is released by on the sending side after serializing, and deserialized events carry a fresh claim, so the fetching side sees the same contract as a local fetch.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Write.GroupedWriteBaseOperator
files.Summary: An egress operator that groups rows by key.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Operators.Write.SimpleGroupedWriteOperator
files.Summary: Helper class to implement a write operator that groups values by primary keys.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode.DistributedPlanModifier
files.Summary:
Splits a not-yet-distributed plan into substreams. Sink roots are assigned round robin, and partitionable operators (merge joins, aggregates, window functions) are split into one copy per substream by . Existing exchange relations (e.g. from distributed views) are placed in one consumer's substream; consumers in other substreams get cross-substream targets and references.

Requires an optimized plan (only merge joins can be partitioned) and runs as the last optimizer step when is set. Deterministic, so each substream host can compute the plan independently.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.Optimizer.DistributedMode.DistributedPlanOptions
files.Summary: Options for that controls how a plan is split into substreams.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.RowEvent
files.Summary: Basic row event

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.SpiceDbConnectorManagerExtensions
files.Summary: Provides extension methods on to register SpiceDB relationship sources and sinks in a Flowtide stream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Core.StreamEventBatch
files.Summary: Represents a batch of stream events. A schema describes the name of the columns that is used for all events in this batch. The schema does not contain the data type in the column, since that can differ between events.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrategies.CustomExceptionStrategy
files.Summary: An that delegates stream failure notifications to a caller-supplied callback.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrategies.ExitProcessStrategy
files.Summary: An that terminates the current process gracefully when the stream encounters an unhandled failure.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Engine.FailureStrategies.KillProcessStrategy
files.Summary: An that immediately terminates the current process when the stream encounters an unhandled failure.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.AppendTree.IAppendTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer>
files.Summary: A forward, read-only cursor over the entries of an . must be called to position the cursor before enumerating; iterating without seeking first throws. Once positioned, enumerate the iterator to walk entries in ascending key order from the seek position onward.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Comparers.LongComparer
files.Summary: Compares values in ascending order. Used as the element comparer for keys stored in the trees and key containers, for example wrapped inside a list comparer or passed to a key container binary search.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Comparers.PrimitiveListComparer<T>
files.Summary: A B+ tree comparer for keys that are unmanaged, naturally comparable values stored in a . It orders keys using their implementation and locates them with a binary search over the container's contiguous buffer.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.CompressionMethod
files.Summary: The granularity at which compression is applied to state pages.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.CompressionType
files.Summary: The algorithm used to compress state pages before they are written to the file cache or persistent storage.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures.BitmapList
files.Summary: A growable list of bits packed into 32-bit words and backed by unmanaged memory allocated from an . It supports the usual list operations (add, get, set, insert and remove) as well as range and batch variants that shift large blocks of bits at once, using vectorized shifts where available. It is commonly used as a validity (null) mask for columnar data. Instances own native memory and must be disposed; releases the buffer and returns the instance to for reuse.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.DataStructures.PrimitiveList<T>
files.Summary: A growable list of unmanaged values stored in native memory allocated from an . It behaves like a list (add, insert, remove, index) but keeps its elements in a contiguous unmanaged buffer, which can be exposed as a or and serialized without copying element by element. Ownership is reference counted through and , and the buffer is released when the list is disposed or the last reference is returned.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.FasterKV.DeviceFactories.SegmentSizeInitializeDeviceFactory
files.Summary: Helper device factory that initializes the device with a segment size. This can be useful to limit the writing size, such as with Azure blob storage.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.IAppendTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer>
files.Summary: A B+ tree that only supports appending entries in strictly increasing key order, which keeps all writes in the rightmost leaf and avoids the node splitting a general purpose tree needs. It is suited to time ordered data such as a time series, where new entries always have a larger key than everything already stored. Data is paged through a state client so the tree can grow beyond memory and be persisted across checkpoints.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Memory.GlobalMemoryManager
files.Summary: Memory manager that is global in a process, should not be used as much as possible since the memory allocated with this does not contribute to metric gathering from a stream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Memory.IOperatorMemoryManager
files.Summary: Used in an operator to manage memory allocation and deallocation. Exist in operator level to allow gathering of metrics how much memory each operator uses.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Memory.IStreamMemoryManager
files.Summary: Manages the memory for one stream. Can be used to keep track of total memory used in a stream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Mimalloc.MiMalloc
files.Summary: https://github.com/microsoft/mimalloc

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir.CheckpointVersion
files.Summary: Contains version information of a checkpoint and also if that version is a snapshot.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir.IReservoirBuilder
files.Summary: Allows configuring the reservoir storage options, such as cache settings, snapshot intervals, and storage providers. This interface is used during the setup of the reservoir storage to specify how the reservoir should manage its data storage and caching behavior.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir.MemoryDisk.MemoryFileProvider
files.Summary: An implementation of IFileStorageProvider that uses in-memory storage for checkpoint and data files. This is primarily intended for testing purposes, allowing for fast and efficient storage operations without the overhead of disk I/O. The MemoryFileProvider can be used to simulate file storage behavior in unit tests or other scenarios where a lightweight, in-memory storage solution is sufficient.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Persistence.Reservoir.StorageProviderContext
files.Summary: Provides contextual information supplied to an during initialization.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.SerializableObject
files.Summary: A value handed to a file cache or persistent storage session to be written. It carries the data in one of two forms so callers do not have to serialize ahead of time: either a live together with the that knows how to serialize it, or a block of bytes that has already been serialized. The bytes form is used when data is copied straight from the file cache to persistent storage, or for already serialized blobs such as metadata, where re-serialization is not needed.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.SqlServer.SqlServerBulkCopySettings
files.Summary: Represents the settings for SQL Server bulk copy operations.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.SqlServer.SqlServerPersistentStorageSettings
files.Summary: Represents the settings for SQL Server persistent storage.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateManager.IObjectState<T>
files.Summary: Represent a state that stores an object.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StateSerializeOptions
files.Summary: Controls how state pages are serialized to the file cache and persistent storage, mainly how they are compressed. An instance left at its defaults applies no compression. Configure it through the storage builder, for example with ZstdPageCompression or NoCompression.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.StreamVersionInformation
files.Summary: Identifies the version of a running stream so that persisted state can be matched to the plan that produced it. It is supplied through the stream builder (SetVersionInformation) and flows into storage initialization, where providers use it to detect plan changes between restarts and to scope stored data to a particular version.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.BulkSearchKeyResult
files.Summary: The result of searching one key within a leaf during a bulk search: which key it was and the index range it matched in that leaf.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.FindBoundriesResult
files.Summary: The inclusive index range of the entries in a key container that match a searched key. When the key is not present both bounds are the bitwise complement of the index where it would be inserted.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.GenericWriteFunction<V>
files.Summary: A read-modify-write callback invoked for a key during a write. It receives the value supplied to the write call, the current stored value (or default when absent), and whether a value currently exists, and returns the new value together with the to apply.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.GenericWriteOperation
files.Summary: The change a read-modify-write applied to a key.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTree<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer>
files.Summary: A persistent B+ tree mapping keys to values. Keys and values are held in containers and the tree is paged through a state client, so it can grow beyond memory, offload to storage and be persisted across checkpoints. Reads and writes are asynchronous because a lookup may need to load pages from storage.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTreeBulkInserter<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer>
files.Summary: Applies a whole batch of rows to a B+ tree in a single pass. The batch is sorted by key and grouped by the leaf each row lands in, so each affected leaf is loaded once and any required node splits and merges are handled together. This is more efficient than upserting rows one at a time when writing a large batch. Each row's effect (insert, update, delete or no change) is decided by an .

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTreeIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer>
files.Summary: A cursor over a B+ tree that enumerates the tree one leaf page at a time. Position it first with or , then enumerate it to walk the pages from that position onward; each yielded exposes the entries of a leaf.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTreeKeySerializer<K, TKeyContainer>
files.Summary: Serializes and deserializes the keys of a B+ tree node, converting a node's key container to and from the bytes stored for that page. It also has checkpoint and initialize hooks for any serializer level metadata, such as schema information or global dictionaries.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBPlusTreePageIterator<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer>
files.Summary: A view over a single leaf page yielded while iterating a B+ tree. It enumerates the page's key/value entries, exposes the page's and containers, supports write locking, and can persist in-place edits through .

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBplusTreeBulkSearch<K, V, TKeyContainer, TValueContainer, TComparer>
files.Summary: Searches many keys against a B+ tree in a single pass. The keys are sorted and grouped by the leaf they fall in, so each leaf is loaded once and the results are produced one leaf at a time. After , call in a loop and read and for each leaf until it returns false. A key whose match runs to the end of a leaf is carried over into the next leaf. Dispose the searcher to release the leaf page it holds.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBplusTreeComparer<K, TKeyContainer>
files.Summary: Defines how keys are compared and located within a key container for a B+ tree. Implementations can provide a search strategy tailored to how the keys are stored, for example a binary search over a sorted list.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IBplusTreeValueSerializer<V, TValueContainer>
files.Summary: Serializes and deserializes the values of a B+ tree node, converting a node's value container to and from the bytes stored for that page. It also has checkpoint and initialize hooks for any serializer level metadata, such as schema information or global dictionaries.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IKeyContainer<K>
files.Summary: Stores the keys of a single B+ tree node. Implementations choose how keys are laid out, for example a managed list or a contiguous unmanaged buffer, and the tree uses this abstraction to search, insert, update and remove keys within a node. Keys within a container are kept in sorted order.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IRowMutator<K, V>
files.Summary: Decides, per row, how a bulk insert applies an incoming row to a B+ tree. The bulk inserter calls for each row to merge the incoming value with any existing value and choose the operation to perform, and to estimate the serialized size of a run of rows for split planning.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Storage.Tree.IValueContainer<V>
files.Summary: Stores the values of a single B+ tree node, in the same order as the keys in the node's key container. Implementations choose how values are laid out, for example a managed list or a contiguous unmanaged buffer, and the tree uses this abstraction to read, insert, update and remove values within a node.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.CustomProtobuf.CustomProtoReflection
files.Summary: Holder for reflection information generated from CustomProto/custom_proto.proto

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.CustomProtobuf.PullBucketExchangeTarget
files.Summary: Packed into the extended field of an exchange target when the target stores its data in a bucket that is pulled by the target.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.CustomProtobuf.SubstreamExchangeTarget
files.Summary: Packed into the extended field of an exchange target when the target sends its data to another substream.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.CustomProtobuf.TableFunctionRelation.Types
files.Summary: Container for nested types declared in the TableFunctionRelation message type.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Expressions.TableFunction
files.Summary: Represents a table function. A table function can only be used in the FROM clause or in joins.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Relations.IterationReferenceReadRelation
files.Summary: This must be different from reference relation since it must be known if its output should be sent on the loop output or on the egress output from the fixed point vertex.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Relations.NormalizationRelation
files.Summary: A relation that normalizes the input based on a key column. Any update on that key will replace the current value with the new value. A delete will delete the key even though there have been multiple inserts before with different values but the same key.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Relations.StandardOutputExchangeReferenceRelation
files.Summary: Relation that marks that it should get its data from an exchange relations standard output.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.Sql.TableProviderTableFunctionArguments
files.Summary: Carries all SQL-parser context needed by an implementation to resolve a table-valued function call into a Substrait .

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Substrait.SubstraitSerializer
files.Summary: Serializes a into the Substrait protobuf format or its JSON representation, the counterpart of . A serialized plan is how plans travel between processes, for example to Orleans grains, and a deserialization always produces a fresh plan instance, which matters because building a stream mutates the plan in place.

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.TestFramework.StreamTestHttpMonitor
files.Summary: Monitors a stream for updates by using the testInformation endpoint

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Zanzibar.QueryPlanner.Models.ZanzibarJoinOnUserTypeId
files.Summary: Join that joins on "l.user_type = r.user_type and l.user_id = r.user_id"

files.Title: FlowtideDotNet.Zanzibar.QueryPlanner.Models.ZanzibarJoinUserToObject
files.Summary: Join that joins on "l.user_type = r.object_type AND l.user_id = r.object_id"
